steps.title: Use a nonnegative variable
steps.content:
The right side must be nonnegative, so let
$$t=31-x$$
Then $t\ge0$ and
$$x=31-t$$

steps.title: Rewrite with one variable
steps.content:
Substitute $x=31-t$ into the equation.
$$\sqrt{k-(31-t)}=t$$
Squaring is valid because $t\ge0$.
$$k-31+t=t^2$$
$$k=t^2-t+31$$

steps.title: Minimize the constant
steps.content:
Complete the square.
$$k=t^2-t+31=(t-\frac{1}{2})^2+\frac{123}{4}$$
The minimum value is $k=\frac{123}{4}$, so
$$4k=4\cdot\frac{123}{4}=123$$
The answer is 123.
